# UC-22.12.3 · Segregation of duties conflicts across SAP / Oracle financial roles

## Description

Supports SOX IT general and application controls evidence for "Segregation of duties conflicts across SAP / Oracle financial roles" by correlating security, change, ERP, and ITSM telemetry with control ownership records, strengthening SOX §404 attestation.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for SOX ITGC compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes erp and sourcetypes sap:audit. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures. Indexes required: `index=erp`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Active Directory (3207), Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)..
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `WinEventLog:Security`, Active Directory replication/audit, `snow:*`, SAP/Oracle audit, `mssql:audit`, PAM, backup jobs.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=erp sourcetype=sap:audit earliest=-30d
| eval uc_tag="22.12.3"
| stats values(agr_name) as roles by uname
| eval role_count=mvcount(roles)
| lookup sap_sod_matrix.csv uname OUTPUT conflict_flag
| where conflict_flag="true" OR role_count>6
| table uname, roles, conflict_flag
```

Understanding this SPL

**Segregation of duties conflicts across SAP / Oracle financial roles (SOX §404)** — Supports SOX IT general and application controls evidence for "Segregation of duties conflicts across SAP / Oracle financial roles" by correlating security, change, ERP, and ITSM telemetry with control ownership records, strengthening SOX §404 attestation.

Documented **Data sources**: `WinEventLog:Security`, Active Directory replication/audit, `snow:*`, SAP/Oracle audit, `mssql:audit`, PAM, backup jobs. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Active Directory (3207), Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: erp; **sourcetype**: sap:audit. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=erp, sourcetype=sap:audit, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **uc_tag** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `stats` aggregates events by **uname**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **role_count** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where conflict_flag="true" OR role_count>6` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Output fields: conflict_flag, role_count, roles, uc_tag, uname.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ERP audit trail (SAP, Oracle ERP), reconcile to ITSM or change records where needed, and confirm results, field extractions, and index permissions are correct.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time chart, Table, Single value, Bar chart

## SPL

```spl
index=erp sourcetype=sap:audit earliest=-30d
| eval uc_tag="22.12.3"
| stats values(agr_name) as roles by uname
| eval role_count=mvcount(roles)
| lookup sap_sod_matrix.csv uname OUTPUT conflict_flag
| where conflict_flag="true" OR role_count>6
| table uname, roles, conflict_flag
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.user, All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Time chart, Table, Single value, Bar chart. Key fields: `conflict_flag`, `role_count`, `roles`, `uc_tag`, `uname`.
